ip_outcome_note: The card name is 'Ninja (XIV)' with game tag ffxiv and category job-class in frontmatter. The FFXIV Ninja is an established job class in Final Fantasy XIV introduced in the Heavensward-era expansion. The depicted figure's dark layered armor, face mask, and dual-blade ready stance are consistent with FFXIV Ninja job visual design per the official FFXIV job guide. This is a job-class card, not a named individual. IP is Square Enix-internal. ip_verified set to true.

trivia_bullets.text: Opus VIII was the first FFTCG set to feature Final Fantasy XIV: Stormblood content. The Ninja (XIV) card (8-057C) is among the set's FFXIV job-class Backup cards, which Opus VIII introduced alongside FFVII and FFXV content [Square Enix FFTCG Opus VIII release page].
sources: Square Enix FFTCG Opus VIII release page (fftcg.square-enix-games.com/na/release/opus-viii)

trivia_bullets.text: Ninja (8-057C) is a cost-2 Wind Backup. Activating (Wind)(Wind)(Dull) and sacrificing the Ninja to the Break Zone lets you choose 1 Backup of cost 3 or less and destroy it — a Backup-removal tool that trades card advantage for tempo. The 8-057C and its Opus V counterpart (5-017C) both see play outside pure Ninja-archetype decks for this flexible removal [Search snippet from French FFTCG card database fftcg.fr; fftcgmognet.com community reference].

In FFXIV lore, the Ninja job class uses 'ninjutsu' — formed by combining up to three mudra (ritual hand gestures) that manipulate vital energies. The official FFXIV job description states: 'Hailing from the war-torn lands of the Far East, the secret arts of the ninja were born of necessity.' The job was added to the game during A Realm Reborn's development cycle and became one of FFXIV's most mechanically layered jobs [FFXIV Official Job Guide: Ninja (na.finalfantasyxiv.com/jobguide/ninja)].

The FFTCG Ninja job-class card has appeared across multiple Opus sets (2-114C, 5-017C, 8-057C, 9-017C), each drawing from a different Final Fantasy title's Ninja aesthetic. The 8-057C is specifically tagged FFXIV, distinguishing it from the job's earlier printings sourced from older FF titles [FFTCG card database cross-reference via search].
sources: FFTCG community database via WebSearch cross-reference
